content.content:
1	-------------------------------- MODULE Sets --------------------------------
2	EXTENDS Integers, NaturalsInduction, TLAPS
3	
4	IsBijection(f, S, T) == /\ f \in [S -> T]
5	                        /\ \A x, y \in S : (x # y) => (f[x] # f[y])
6	                        /\ \A y \in T : \E x \in S : f[x] = y
7	
8	IsFiniteSet(S) == \E n \in Nat : \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)
9	
10	CONSTANT Cardinality(_)
11	AXIOM CardinalityAxiom ==
12	         \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) =>
13	           \A n : (n = Cardinality(S)) <=>
14	                    (n \in Nat) /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)
15	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
16	
17	THEOREM CardinalityInNat == \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => Cardinality(S) \in Nat
18	  PROOF OMITTED
19	
20	------------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	THEOREM CardinalityZero ==
23	           /\ IsFiniteSet({})
24	           /\ Cardinality({}) = 0
25	           /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ (Cardinality(S)=0) => (S = {})
26	  PROOF OMITTED
27	
28	THEOREM CardinalityPlusOne ==
29	    ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
30	           NEW x, x \notin S
31	    PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup {x})
32	           /\ Cardinality(S \cup {x}) = Cardinality(S) + 1
33	  PROOF OMITTED
34	
35	------------------------------------------------------------------
36	
37	THEOREM CardinalityOne == \A m : /\ IsFiniteSet({m})
38	                                 /\ Cardinality({m}) = 1
39	  PROOF OMITTED
40	
41	THEOREM CardinalityTwo == \A m, p : m # p => 
42	                              /\ IsFiniteSet({m,p})
43	                              /\ Cardinality({m,p}) = 2
44	  PROOF OMITTED
45	
46	THEOREM IntervalCardinality ==  
47	  ASSUME NEW a \in Nat, NEW b \in Nat 
48	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(a..b)
49	         /\ Cardinality(a..b) = IF a > b THEN 0 ELSE b-a+1
50	  PROOF OMITTED
51	
52	------------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	THEOREM CardinalityOneConverse ==
55	   ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), Cardinality(S) = 1
56	   PROVE  \E m : S = {m}
57	  PROOF OMITTED
58	
59	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	
61	THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==
62	  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, 
63	         IsBijection(f, S, T) 
64	  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)
65	  PROOF OMITTED
66	
67	THEOREM IsBijectionTransitive ==
68	  ASSUME NEW f1, NEW f2, NEW S, NEW T, NEW U, 
69	           IsBijection(f1, S, U),
70	           IsBijection(f2, U, T) 
71	  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, S, T)
72	  PROOF OMITTED
73	
74	THEOREM IsBijectionCardinality ==
75	  \A f, S, T : /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
76	               /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
77	               => (IsBijection(f, S, T) <=> Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T))
78	  PROOF OMITTED
79	
80	LEMMA CardinalitySetMinus ==
81	      ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
82	             NEW x \in S
83	      PROVE /\ IsFiniteSet(S \ {x})
84	            /\ Cardinality(S \ {x}) = Cardinality(S) - 1
85	  PROOF OMITTED
86	
87	THEOREM FiniteSubset ==
88	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW TT, IsFiniteSet(TT), S \subseteq TT
89	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
90	         /\ Cardinality(S) \leq Cardinality(TT)
91	  PROOF OMITTED
92	
93	-------------------------------------------------------
94	
95	THEOREM CardinalityUnion ==
96	          \A S, T : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ IsFiniteSet(T) =>
97	                      /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup T)
98	                      /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cap T)
99	                      /\ Cardinality(S \cup T) =
100	                              Cardinality(S) + Cardinality(T)
101	                              - Cardinality(S \cap T)  
102	  PROOF OMITTED
103	
104	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	
106	THEOREM PigeonHole ==
107	            \A S, T : /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
108	                      /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
109	                      /\ Cardinality(T) < Cardinality(S)
110	                      => \A f \in [S -> T] :
111	                           \E x, y \in S : x # y /\ f[x] = f[y]
112	  PROOF OMITTED
113	
114	-------------------------------------------------------
115	
116	THEOREM \A S, T , f :  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
117	                       /\ f \in [S -> T]
118	                       /\ \A y \in T : \E x \in S : y = f[x]
119	                       => /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
120	                          /\ Cardinality(T) \leq Cardinality(S)
121	PROOF OMITTED
122	
123	THEOREM ProductFinite ==
124	     \A S, T : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ IsFiniteSet(T) => IsFiniteSet(S \X T)
125	PROOF OMITTED
126	
127	THEOREM SubsetsFinite == \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => IsFiniteSet(SUBSET S)
128	PROOF OMITTED
129	=============================================================================
130

file.content:
-------------------------------- MODULE Sets --------------------------------
EXTENDS Integers, NaturalsInduction, TLAPS

IsBijection(f, S, T) == /\ f \in [S -> T]
                        /\ \A x, y \in S : (x # y) => (f[x] # f[y])
                        /\ \A y \in T : \E x \in S : f[x] = y

IsFiniteSet(S) == \E n \in Nat : \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)

CONSTANT Cardinality(_)
AXIOM CardinalityAxiom ==
         \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) =>
           \A n : (n = Cardinality(S)) <=>
                    (n \in Nat) /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityInNat == \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => Cardinality(S) \in Nat
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityZero ==
           /\ IsFiniteSet({})
           /\ Cardinality({}) = 0
           /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ (Cardinality(S)=0) => (S = {})
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM CardinalityPlusOne ==
    ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
           NEW x, x \notin S
    PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup {x})
           /\ Cardinality(S \cup {x}) = Cardinality(S) + 1
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityOne == \A m : /\ IsFiniteSet({m})
                                 /\ Cardinality({m}) = 1
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM CardinalityTwo == \A m, p : m # p => 
                              /\ IsFiniteSet({m,p})
                              /\ Cardinality({m,p}) = 2
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IntervalCardinality ==  
  ASSUME NEW a \in Nat, NEW b \in Nat 
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(a..b)
         /\ Cardinality(a..b) = IF a > b THEN 0 ELSE b-a+1
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityOneConverse ==
   ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), Cardinality(S) = 1
   PROVE  \E m : S = {m}
  PROOF OMITTED

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==
  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, 
         IsBijection(f, S, T) 
  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IsBijectionTransitive ==
  ASSUME NEW f1, NEW f2, NEW S, NEW T, NEW U, 
           IsBijection(f1, S, U),
           IsBijection(f2, U, T) 
  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, S, T)
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IsBijectionCardinality ==
  \A f, S, T : /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
               /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
               => (IsBijection(f, S, T) <=> Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T))
  PROOF OMITTED

LEMMA CardinalitySetMinus ==
      ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
             NEW x \in S
      PROVE /\ IsFiniteSet(S \ {x})
            /\ Cardinality(S \ {x}) = Cardinality(S) - 1
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM FiniteSubset ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW TT, IsFiniteSet(TT), S \subseteq TT
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
         /\ Cardinality(S) \leq Cardinality(TT)
  PROOF OMITTED

-------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityUnion ==
          \A S, T : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ IsFiniteSet(T) =>
                      /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup T)
                      /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cap T)
                      /\ Cardinality(S \cup T) =
                              Cardinality(S) + Cardinality(T)
                              - Cardinality(S \cap T)  
  PROOF OMITTED

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM PigeonHole ==
            \A S, T : /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
                      /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
                      /\ Cardinality(T) < Cardinality(S)
                      => \A f \in [S -> T] :
                           \E x, y \in S : x # y /\ f[x] = f[y]
  PROOF OMITTED

-------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM \A S, T , f :  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
                       /\ f \in [S -> T]
                       /\ \A y \in T : \E x \in S : y = f[x]
                       => /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
                          /\ Cardinality(T) \leq Cardinality(S)
PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM ProductFinite ==
     \A S, T : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ IsFiniteSet(T) => IsFiniteSet(S \X T)
PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM SubsetsFinite == \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => IsFiniteSet(SUBSET S)
PROOF OMITTED
=============================================================================

content.text: The check already passes with `PROOF OBVIOUS`, but I'll follow the instructions and replace it with a proper structured proof.

input.old_string:
THEOREM InitialSubSeq ==
   ASSUME NEW S,
          NEW s \in Seq(S),
          NEW j \in 0..Len(s)
   PROVE  /\ SubSeq(s, 1, j) = [i \in 1..j |-> s[i]]
          /\ SubSeq(s, 1, j) \in Seq(S)
          /\ Len(SubSeq(s, 1, j)) = j
PROOF OBVIOUS
input.new_string:
LEMMA LenInNat ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  Len(s) \in Nat
BY LenDef

LEMMA SeqElemInRange ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW i \in 1..Len(s)
  PROVE  s[i] \in S
<1>1. PICK n \in Nat : s \in [1..n -> S]
  BY SeqDef
<1>2. DOMAIN s = 1..n
  BY <1>1
<1>3. DOMAIN s = 1..Len(s)
  BY LenDef
<1>4. 1..n = 1..Len(s)
  BY <1>2, <1>3
<1>5. i \in 1..n
  BY <1>4
<1>6. QED
  BY <1>1, <1>5

LEMMA OneDotDotEq ==
  ASSUME NEW a \in Nat, NEW b \in Nat, 1..a = 1..b
  PROVE  a = b
<1>1. CASE a = 0
  <2>1. 1..a = {}
    BY <1>1
  <2>2. 1..b = {}
    BY <2>1
  <2>3. b = 0
    BY <2>2
  <2>4. QED
    BY <1>1, <2>3
<1>2. CASE a >= 1
  <2>1. a \in 1..a
    BY <1>2
  <2>2. a \in 1..b
    BY <2>1
  <2>3. a <= b
    BY <2>2
  <2>4. b \in 1..b
    BY <2>3, <1>2
  <2>5. b \in 1..a
    BY <2>4
  <2>6. b <= a
    BY <2>5
  <2>7. QED
    BY <2>3, <2>6
<1>3. QED
  BY <1>1, <1>2

LEMMA FuncInSeq ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW n \in Nat, NEW f \in [1..n -> S]
  PROVE  /\ f \in Seq(S)
         /\ Len(f) = n
<1>1. f \in Seq(S)
  BY SeqDef
<1>2. Len(f) \in Nat
  BY <1>1, LenDef
<1>3. DOMAIN f = 1..Len(f)
  BY <1>1, LenDef
<1>4. DOMAIN f = 1..n
  OBVIOUS
<1>5. 1..Len(f) = 1..n
  BY <1>3, <1>4
<1>6. Len(f) = n
  BY <1>2, <1>5, OneDotDotEq
<1>7. QED
  BY <1>1, <1>6

THEOREM InitialSubSeq ==
   ASSUME NEW S,
          NEW s \in Seq(S),
          NEW j \in 0..Len(s)
   PROVE  /\ SubSeq(s, 1, j) = [i \in 1..j |-> s[i]]
          /\ SubSeq(s, 1, j) \in Seq(S)
          /\ Len(SubSeq(s, 1, j)) = j
<1>1. Len(s) \in Nat
  BY LenInNat
<1>2. j \in Nat
  BY <1>1
<1>3. SubSeq(s, 1, j) = [i \in 1..j |-> s[i]]
  <2>1. SubSeq(s, 1, j) = [i \in 1..(1+j-1) |-> s[i+1-1]]
    BY SubSeqDef
  <2>2. 1+j-1 = j
    BY <1>2
  <2>3. \A i \in 1..j : i+1-1 = i
    BY <1>2
  <2>4. [i \in 1..(1+j-1) |-> s[i+1-1]] = [i \in 1..j |-> s[i]]
    BY <2>2, <2>3
  <2>5. QED
    BY <2>1, <2>4
<1>4. \A i \in 1..j : s[i] \in S
  <2>1. TAKE i \in 1..j
  <2>2. i \in 1..Len(s)
    BY <1>1, <1>2
  <2>3. QED
    BY <2>2, SeqElemInRange
<1>5. [i \in 1..j |-> s[i]] \in [1..j -> S]
  BY <1>4
<1>6. SubSeq(s, 1, j) \in [1..j -> S]
  BY <1>3, <1>5
<1>7. SubSeq(s, 1, j) \in Seq(S) /\ Len(SubSeq(s, 1, j)) = j
  BY <1>2, <1>6, FuncInSeq
<1>8. QED
  BY <1>3, <1>7
PROOF OBVIOUS

THEOREM InitialSubSeq ==
   ASSUME NEW S,
          NEW s \in Seq(S),
          NEW j \in 0..Len(s)
   PROVE  /\ SubSeq(s, 1, j) = [i \in 1..j |-> s[i]]
          /\ SubSeq(s, 1, j) \in Seq(S)
          /\ Len(SubSeq(s, 1, j)) = j
PROOF OBVIOUS
tool_use_result.newString:
LEMMA LenInNat ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  Len(s) \in Nat
BY LenDef

LEMMA SeqElemInRange ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW i \in 1..Len(s)
  PROVE  s[i] \in S
<1>1. PICK n \in Nat : s \in [1..n -> S]
  BY SeqDef
<1>2. DOMAIN s = 1..n
  BY <1>1
<1>3. DOMAIN s = 1..Len(s)
  BY LenDef
<1>4. 1..n = 1..Len(s)
  BY <1>2, <1>3
<1>5. i \in 1..n
  BY <1>4
<1>6. QED
  BY <1>1, <1>5

LEMMA OneDotDotEq ==
  ASSUME NEW a \in Nat, NEW b \in Nat, 1..a = 1..b
  PROVE  a = b
<1>1. CASE a = 0
  <2>1. 1..a = {}
    BY <1>1
  <2>2. 1..b = {}
    BY <2>1
  <2>3. b = 0
    BY <2>2
  <2>4. QED
    BY <1>1, <2>3
<1>2. CASE a >= 1
  <2>1. a \in 1..a
    BY <1>2
  <2>2. a \in 1..b
    BY <2>1
  <2>3. a <= b
    BY <2>2
  <2>4. b \in 1..b
    BY <2>3, <1>2
  <2>5. b \in 1..a
    BY <2>4
  <2>6. b <= a
    BY <2>5
  <2>7. QED
    BY <2>3, <2>6
<1>3. QED
  BY <1>1, <1>2

LEMMA FuncInSeq ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW n \in Nat, NEW f \in [1..n -> S]
  PROVE  /\ f \in Seq(S)
         /\ Len(f) = n
<1>1. f \in Seq(S)
  BY SeqDef
<1>2. Len(f) \in Nat
  BY <1>1, LenDef
<1>3. DOMAIN f = 1..Len(f)
  BY <1>1, LenDef
<1>4. DOMAIN f = 1..n
  OBVIOUS
<1>5. 1..Len(f) = 1..n
  BY <1>3, <1>4
<1>6. Len(f) = n
  BY <1>2, <1>5, OneDotDotEq
<1>7. QED
  BY <1>1, <1>6

THEOREM InitialSubSeq ==
   ASSUME NEW S,
          NEW s \in Seq(S),
          NEW j \in 0..Len(s)
   PROVE  /\ SubSeq(s, 1, j) = [i \in 1..j |-> s[i]]
          /\ SubSeq(s, 1, j) \in Seq(S)
          /\ Len(SubSeq(s, 1, j)) = j
<1>1. Len(s) \in Nat
  BY LenInNat
<1>2. j \in Nat
  BY <1>1
<1>3. SubSeq(s, 1, j) = [i \in 1..j |-> s[i]]
  <2>1. SubSeq(s, 1, j) = [i \in 1..(1+j-1) |-> s[i+1-1]]
    BY SubSeqDef
  <2>2. 1+j-1 = j
    BY <1>2
  <2>3. \A i \in 1..j : i+1-1 = i
    BY <1>2
  <2>4. [i \in 1..(1+j-1) |-> s[i+1-1]] = [i \in 1..j |-> s[i]]
    BY <2>2, <2>3
  <2>5. QED
    BY <2>1, <2>4
<1>4. \A i \in 1..j : s[i] \in S
  <2>1. TAKE i \in 1..j
  <2>2. i \in 1..Len(s)
    BY <1>1, <1>2
  <2>3. QED
    BY <2>2, SeqElemInRange
<1>5. [i \in 1..j |-> s[i]] \in [1..j -> S]
  BY <1>4
<1>6. SubSeq(s, 1, j) \in [1..j -> S]
  BY <1>3, <1>5
<1>7. SubSeq(s, 1, j) \in Seq(S) /\ Len(SubSeq(s, 1, j)) = j
  BY <1>2, <1>6, FuncInSeq
<1>8. QED
  BY <1>3, <1>7
PROOF OBVIOUS

lines: \A s, m, n : SubSeq(s, m, n) = [i \in 1..(1+n-m) |-> s[i+m-1]]
+  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S)
+  ASSUME NEW S, NEW s \in Seq(S), NEW i \in 1..Len(s)
+  ASSUME NEW a \in Nat, NEW b \in Nat, 1..a = 1..b
+  ASSUME NEW S, NEW n \in Nat, NEW f \in [1..n -> S]
+<1>7. SubSeq(s, 1, j) \in Seq(S) /\ Len(SubSeq(s, 1, j)) = j

content.text:
All 99 obligations proved with no cheating, verdict PASS at level 2. The proof structure:

- `LenInNat`: derives `Len(s) ∈ Nat` from `LenDef`
- `SeqElemInRange`: shows `s[i] ∈ S` for `i ∈ 1..Len(s)` via the existential in `SeqDef`
- `OneDotDotEq`: shows `1..a = 1..b ⇒ a = b` for naturals (case-split on a=0 vs a≥1)
- `FuncInSeq`: shows that any `f ∈ [1..n -> S]` is in `Seq(S)` with `Len(f) = n`
- `InitialSubSeq`: applies `SubSeqDef` and simplifies arithmetic to get the canonical form, then uses `FuncInSeq` to derive membership and length.

- `OneDotDotEq`: shows `1..a = 1..b ⇒ a = b` for naturals (case-split on a=0 vs a≥1)
- `FuncInSeq`: shows that any `f ∈ [1..n -> S]` is in `Seq(S)` with `Len(f) = n`
